source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/231031 보완/A-TCF-5-152 Quality Documents/AQP712 Risk Management Regulations_REV.5 (ENG).pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: AQP712 Risk Management Regulations Rev. 5 defines company procedures for medical device risk management, including planning, risk analysis, evaluation, control, residual risk assessment, post-production review, and records.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used as a company management standard for risk management tasks to support product quality and safety through risk analysis, evaluation, and management.
keyPoints: The procedure applies to risk management tasks.
The CEO conducts Risk Management Meetings and provides risk management policies and goals.
The quality manager plans and presides over a Risk Management Meeting at least once a year.
Risk management plans must address scope, verification, responsibility allocation, revision requirements, risk control standards, environmental risks, and hazardous substance risk information.
Risk management report traceability must cover risk analysis, risk assessment, implementation and verification of risk control measures, and evaluation of acceptability of residual risk.
Records include FMEA, Risk Management Plan, and Risk Management Report forms stored by the Quality Team.

concepts.name: Risk Management
concepts.evidence: Systematic application of management policies, procedures and regulations to the work of analyzing, evaluating and managing risks

concepts.name: Risk Management File
concepts.evidence: A set of records or documents generated by the risk management process

concepts.name: Risk Analysis
concepts.evidence: The systematic use of available information to identify hazards and estimate risks

concepts.name: Risk Assessment
concepts.evidence: Overall process of risk analysis and evaluation

concepts.name: Risk Control
concepts.evidence: A process by which protective actions are implemented and decisions are made to maintain or reduce risk within a defined range.

concepts.name: Residual Risk
concepts.evidence: Residual risks after safety control measures

concepts.name: Risk Evaluation
concepts.evidence: The process of comparing an estimated risk to a given risk tolerance criterion to determine the acceptability of the risk.

concepts.name: Risk Management Meeting
concepts.evidence: The CEO conducts Risk Management Meetings to ensure the suitability and effectiveness of risk management policies, goals and ISO 14971 standards.

concepts.name: Risk Acceptability Standards
concepts.evidence: Risks are categorized into two areas: Unacceptable Zone Widely acceptable Zone

evidence.claim: AQP712 is titled Risk Management Regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: Standard Name Risk Management Regulations Standard No. AQP712
Date of Revision: 2022.10.31 Risk Management Standards Revision No.: 5

evidence.claim: Revision 5 was a complete revision due to application of EN ISO 14971:2019+A11:2021.
evidence.evidenceText: 2022.10.31 5 Revision of text content and procedures Complete revision due to application of EN ISO 14971:2019+A11:2021

evidence.claim: The purpose is to ensure product quality and safety through risk analysis, evaluation and management.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to be used in management to ensure the quality of products produced by the company and the safety of hazardous substances, and to provide safe products to the customers through risk analysis, evaluation and management.

evidence.claim: The procedure applies to risk management tasks.
evidence.evidenceText: This procedure applies to risk management tasks.

evidence.claim: The CEO conducts Risk Management Meetings for suitability and effectiveness of risk management policies, goals and ISO 14971 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The CEO conducts Risk Management Meetings to ensure the suitability and effectiveness of risk management policies, goals and ISO 14971 standards.

evidence.claim: The quality manager must plan and preside over a Risk Management Meeting at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: The quality manager must plan and preside over a Risk Management Meeting at least once a year

evidence.claim: Risk management plans must comply with policy, goals, and ISO 14971 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The quality team must prepare a risk management plan to implement risk management for the product in compliance with this policy/goal and the requirements of the ISO 14971 standard and obtain approval from the quality manager.

evidence.claim: Risk assessment includes avoiding economic considerations, eliminating ALARP as much as possible, and not using labels as a risk control measure.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk assessment is conducted by reviewing and considering the following cases. ... Disqualification of economic considerations ... Elimination of ALARP as much as possible ... Do not use labels as a risk control measure

evidence.claim: Risk control measures include safe structure by design, packaging, user manual and labeling, and environmental information.
evidence.evidenceText: The quality team leader identifies the following risk control measures ... 1) Safe structure by design: Design 2) Protection measures for the product itself or during the process: Packaging 3) Information about safety: User manual, labeling 4) Information about the environment

evidence.claim: Residual risks are evaluated after implementing risk control measures and recorded in the risk management file.
evidence.evidenceText: The quality team leader evaluates the residual risks that remain after implementing risk control measures according to the Risk Management Plan and records the evaluation record in the risk management file.

evidence.claim: If benefit is greater than risk, proceed and write in the user manual; if benefit is less than risk, it is not allowed.
evidence.evidenceText: (1) Benefit > Risk: Proceed to step 9, write in user manual (2) Benefit < Risk: Not allowed

evidence.claim: Risk management reports must provide traceability for risk analysis, risk assessment, risk control measures, and residual risk acceptability.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk management report must provide traceability of hazards for the following. 1) Risk Analysis 2) Risk Assessment 3) Implementation and verification of risk control measures 4) Evaluation of acceptability of residual risk

evidence.claim: Post-production product or similar product information is collected under the vigilance system of customer complaint handling regulations AQP806.
evidence.evidenceText: The procedure for obtaining and reviewing information about our products or similar products is collected by the quality manager in accordance with the vigilance system of the customer complaint handling regulations (AQP806).

evidence.claim: Risk management uses two risk areas: Widely acceptable Zone and Unacceptable Zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Risks are categorized into two areas: Unacceptable Zone Widely acceptable Zone

evidence.claim: FMEA, Risk Management Plan, and Risk Management Report are Quality Team records stored for 5 years and possessed permanently in archives.
evidence.evidenceText: FMEA (Risk Assessment Table) APF712-1 Quality Team 5 years Permanently Archives Risk Management Plan APF712-2 Quality Team 5 years Permanently Archives Risk Management Report APF712-3 Quality Team 5 years Permanently Archives
